title: Celiac Disease
chief_complaint: I've had diarrhea and bloating for 6 months. I'm losing weight. I'm exhausted all the time. My skin has this itchy blistering rash on my elbows and knees.

physical_exam.Skin: Dermatitis herpetiformis on extensor surfaces (elbows, knees) - grouped vesicles on erythematous base

hidden_data.ttg_iga: Tissue Transglutaminase IgA >100 U/mL (normal <4). Markedly elevated.
hidden_data.iga: Total IgA normal (rules out IgA deficiency)
hidden_data.biopsy: Duodenal biopsy: Villous atrophy (Marsh 3c), crypt hyperplasia, intraepithelial lymphocytosis. Classic for celiac disease.

correct_diagnosis: Celiac Disease with Dermatitis Herpetiformis and Iron Deficiency Anemia
key_learning_points: Celiac disease = autoimmune enteropathy triggered by gluten in genetically susceptible individuals (HLA-DQ2/DQ8)
Classic triad: Diarrhea + weight loss + anemia. Dermatitis herpetiformis is pathognomonic skin manifestation
Diagnosis: tTG-IgA (screening) + EMA (confirmatory) + duodenal biopsy showing villous atrophy. MUST be on gluten-containing diet for testing
Treatment: Strict lifelong gluten-free diet. Iron and vitamin supplementation (B12, folate, vitamin D, calcium)
Refractory celiac = no improvement after 12 months of strict GFD. Increased risk of enteropathy-associated T-cell lymphoma
